summary: Replaced the MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly stub for Ness yoyo item callback it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with matching C. The function calls it_8026B894, then preserves an otherwise unused comparison against ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 by using a volatile pointer expression so the generated code retains the load/compare behavior. The PR author noted the unused comparison made the decompilation tricky but matched 100% locally.
what_changed: Removed the MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly implementation of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk.
Added a C implementation of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk(Item_GObj* gobj, Item_GObj* ref_gobj).

The C implementation obtains the Item pointer with GET_ITEM(gobj), calls it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), and then performs a deliberately unused comparison involving ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and ref_gobj.
Deleted the NOT_IMPLEMENTED placeholder from the non-MWERKS branch and collapsed the conditional assembly/C split.
smart_moves: Used HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 to force the compiler to preserve a load from the owner/reference field even though the comparison result is unused.
Encoded the no-op comparison as (void) ((*owner_ptr == ref_gobj) ? 0 : 0), matching the observed assembly pattern where a cmplw occurs but has no visible semantic effect.
Kept the implementation minimal and close to the original assembly: one setup GET_ITEM, one call to it_8026B894, then the preserved dead comparison.
decomp_lessons: When matching item callbacks, unused comparisons in the original code may still need to be represented explicitly to preserve codegen.
A volatile pointer to a struct field can be useful when the compiler would otherwise remove a load or comparison whose result is unused.
Dead or semantically inert assembly instructions should not automatically be ignored in matching work; they may need carefully written C expressions to reproduce.
For item code, xDD4_itemVar subtype fields can be accessed directly once GET_ITEM(gobj) establishes the concrete Item pointer.
naming_conventions: Kept the existing generated-style callback name it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk.
Used standard item variable names seen in this codebase: gobj, ref_gobj, ip, owner_ptr.
Accessed the Ness yoyo item variable through ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10, preserving the current struct field naming rather than inventing a semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched an inline assembly sequence containing a call to it_8026B894 followed by a load from offset corresponding to xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and a cmplw against ref_gobj.
Used a volatile pointer indirection to prevent optimization from eliminating an otherwise unused field load.
Used a ternary expression with identical outcomes, cast to void, to express a comparison that has no runtime side effect beyond code generation.
Removed MWERKS_GEKKO asm guards after achieving a C match.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/comment states: "The unused comparison made this one a bit tricky, but it matches 100% on my end."
follow_up_queries: Find other item callbacks with unused comparisons after helper calls that may need volatile pointer matching tactics.
Search for remaining MWERKS_GEKKO asm blocks in src/melee/it/items that only preserve dead loads or comparisons.
Look for other uses of xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 to infer whether it has a better semantic name than x10.
